passage: A district literacy coordinator is selecting one reading program to expand next year. The coordinator wants to prioritize the program that produced the strongest benchmark performance while also relying on a similarly sized student sample.
question: Which choice best identifies the program most supported by the table for expansion under the coordinator's criteria?

choices.A: Program A, because it sampled at least 120 students and had the second-highest benchmark rate.
choices.B: Program B, because it has the highest benchmark rate and a sample size comparable to the other programs.
choices.C: Program C, because it sampled the most students regardless of benchmark performance.
choices.D: Program D, because its lower benchmark rate suggests more room for future growth.

explanation: Program B has the highest percentage of students meeting the growth benchmark (69%), and its sample size (118) is in the same range as the others. That most directly matches the coordinator's stated criteria.
